2605.23904.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
2605.23904.paper_summary: Agent skills today are hand-crafted, generated one-shot, or evolved through loosely controlled self-revision, none of which behaves like a deep-learning optimizer for the skill, and none of which reliably improves over its starting point under feedback. We argue the skill should instead be trained as the external state of a frozen agent, with the same discipline that makes weight-space optimization reproducible. SkillOpt is, to our knowledge, the first systematic controllable text-space optimizer for agent skills: a separate optimizer model turns scored rollouts into bounded add/delete/replace edits on a single skill document, and an edit is accepted only when it strictly improves a held-out validation score. A textual learning-rate budget, rejected-edit buffer, and epoch-wise slow/meta update make skill training stable while adding zero inference-time model calls at deployment. Across six benchmarks, seven target models, and three execution harnesses (direct chat, Codex, Claude Code), SkillOpt is best or tied on all 52 evaluated (model, benchmark, harness) cells and beats every per-cell competitor among human, one-shot LLM, Trace2Skill, TextGrad, GEPA, and EvoSkill skills. On GPT-5.5 it lifts the average no-skill accuracy by +23.5 points in direct chat, by +24.8 inside the Codex agentic loop, and by +19.1 inside Claude Code. Transfer experiments further show that optimized skill artifacts retain value when moved across model scales, between Codex and Claude Code execution environments, and to a nearby math benchmark without further optimization.
2605.23904.paper_summary_zh: 目前的代理技能是手工製作的、一次性生成的，或是通過鬆散控制的自我修訂進化而來，這些都不具備深度學習優化器的特性，也都無法在反饋下可靠地改善其起始點。我們主張，技能應該作為一個凍結代理的外部狀態進行訓練，採用使權重空間優化可重複的相同紀律。SkillOpt是我們所知的第一個系統性可控文本空間優化器，用於代理技能：一個單獨的優化器模型將得分的回合轉換為對單一技能文檔的有界添加/刪除/替換編輯，並且只有在嚴格改善保留的驗證分數時，編輯才會被接受。文本學習率預算、被拒絕的編輯緩衝區和每個時期的緩慢/元更新使得技能訓練穩定，同時在部署時不增加任何推理時間模型調用。在六個基準、七個目標模型和三個執行環境（直接聊天、Codex、Claude Code）中，SkillOpt在所有52個評估的（模型、基準、環境）單元中表現最佳或並列最佳，並且在每個單元中超越了人類、一次性LLM、Trace2Skill、TextGrad、GEPA和EvoSkill技能的所有競爭對手。在GPT-5.5上，它將直接聊天中的平均無技能準確率提高了+23.5分，在Codex代理環路內提高了+24.8分，在Claude Code內提高了+19.1分。轉移實驗進一步顯示，優化的技能工件在不同模型規模之間、在Codex和Claude Code執行環境之間，以及在不進一步優化的情況下轉移到相近的數學基準時仍然保留價值。
2605.23904.author: Yifan Yang et.al.
2605.23904.authors: Yifan Yang, Ziyang Gong, Weiquan Huang, Qihao Yang, Ziwei Zhou, Zisu Huang, Yan Li, Xuemei Gao, Qi Dai, Bei Liu, Kai Qiu, Yuqing Yang, Dongdong Chen, Xue Yang, Chong Luo
